question: Whose Mars observation data did Kepler use to derive his laws of planetary motion, and why was that data essential?
notes: Source: Ch.3 §3.1; Brahe gave Kepler decades of precise positional data, Kepler analyzed Mars data to discover elliptical orbits. PDF pages verified by pdftotext.

question: How did Kepler discover his third law, and what relationship does it describe?
notes: Source: Ch.3 §3.1; P² ∝ a³ relating orbital period to average distance. PDF pages verified by pdftotext.

question: What was Newton's central insight about Earth's gravity that allowed him to develop the universal law of gravitation?
notes: Source: Ch.3 §3.3; gravity extends to Moon, inverse-square law unifies celestial and terrestrial motion. PDF pages verified by pdftotext.

question: In Chapter 3, how do Kepler's three laws of planetary motion lay the foundation that Newton's universal law of gravitation later extends, and what does Newton's reformulation of Kepler's third law add?
notes: Source: spans Ch.3 §3.1 (Kepler's three laws, p.87-91), §3.3 (Newton's universal gravitation as the underlying cause, p.95-97), and §3.5 (Newton's reformulation adding the mass term to Kepler's third law, p.99-101). question: How was Neptune discovered, and why is it described as 'the first planet discovered through mathematics'?
notes: Source: Ch.3 §3.6 ('The Discovery of Neptune'). Adams (1843-1845) and Le Verrier (1846) independently predicted Neptune's position from perturbations in Uranus's orbit; Galle observed it on 1846-09-23. Bucket downgraded from chapter_scoped to factual on audit: answer is contained within a single section. PDF pages verified by pdftotext.

question: How does the redshift concept (introduced earlier in the textbook in the chapter on radiation and spectra) relate to Slipher's and Hubble's later observations of distant galaxies?
notes: Source: spans Ch.5 §5.6 (Doppler effect & redshift definition, PDF pages ~185-189) AND Ch.26 (Slipher's spiral nebulae redshift observations + Lemaître/Hubble expanding-universe interpretation, PDF pages ~920-924). The Ch.26 text contains an explicit 'Recall from the chapter on Radiation and Spectra' linkage. System retrieval MUST surface chunks from BOTH chapters — this is the canonical cross_topic test. question: What surprising pattern did Vesto Slipher find when he photographed the spectra of more than 40 spiral nebulae, and why did he have to expose photographic plates for 20 to 40 hours?
notes: Source: Ch.26; Slipher found nearly all spiral spectra were redshifted (one at 1800 km/s); long exposures needed because Lowell's 24-inch refractor was poorly suited to faint nebulae. PDF pages verified by pdftotext (previous range [900,905] was incorrectly using printed page numbers).

question: Describe what an H-R diagram (Hertzsprung-Russell diagram) shows: what are its two axes, who created it, and what does the location of a star on the diagram tell you?
notes: Source: Ch.18 §18.4; H-R diagram is a plot of luminosity vs surface temperature/spectral class, independently devised by Russell (1913) and Hertzsprung (1911). Most stars lie on the main sequence. PDF pages verified by pdftotext.
